The fare calculation relies on a base charge plus extra distance costs.
Every five minutes of waiting time converts to one kilometer of distance.
This rule means the total distance increases when the taxi stops for long periods.
The spring season brings many travelers to the city airport for their trips.
Drivers must calculate the final cost based on the rounded up distance value.
A passenger paying thirty eight yuan implies a specific total distance was traveled.
The relationship between fare and distance is linear after the initial four kilometers.
This system ensures the driver is compensated for both movement and waiting time.
The spring weather often causes traffic delays that add to the final bill.

The actual travel distance was fifteen kilometers but the fare suggests more.
The extra cost indicates that stopping time added at least four kilometers to the trip.
Since five minutes equals one kilometer the maximum stop time is twenty minutes.
This calculation shows how waiting time directly impacts the passenger fare amount.
The driver must explain this conversion rule to avoid confusion with the customer.
The spring season is a busy time for airport shuttles in this city.
Passengers should be aware that stops will increase their total taxi cost.
The math proves that twenty minutes is the maximum possible stop duration here.
Understanding this rule helps everyone plan their travel budget more effectively.
